---

title: "API to Lakehouse ETL"
author: "Fabricio Lennart"
date: "2026-04-03"
format: html
jupyter: python3
execute:
    echo: true
    warning: false
    message: false
---

## Overview

This notebook demonstrates a simple end-to-end data pipeline:

* Extract data from a public API
* Transform the JSON response
* Load the data into a structured format
* Store the result in a Fabric Lakehouse

---

## Step 1: Extract Data from API

```{python}
import requests as rq
import json

url = "https://dummyjson.com/products?limit=200"

def request_data(link: str) -> str:
    response = rq.get(link, timeout=10)
    response.raise_for_status()
    return response.text

data = request_data(url)

data_json = json.loads(data)
products = data_json["products"]
```

---

## Step 2: Transform Data

```{python}
clean_products = [
    {
        "id": p["id"],
        "title": p["title"],
        "price": p["price"]
    }
    for p in products
]

clean_products[:3]
```

---

## Step 3: Load into DataFrame

```{python}
import pandas as pd

df = pd.DataFrame(clean_products)
df.head()
```

---

## Step 4: Save to Lakehouse

```{python}
df.to_csv('../data/products.csv', index=False)
```

---

## Notes

* The Lakehouse acts as a centralized storage layer
* Files are stored under `/Files`
* Suitable for simple ETL pipelines
